multipleChoice.text: Why does Tom say "My! Look behind you, Aunt!" while Aunt Polly is holding the switch?

options.A: He sees something scary behind her.
options.B: He is trying to distract her so he can escape.
options.C: He wants to show her something interesting.
options.D: He is warning her about a falling object.

multipleChoice.text: What does Sid do that gets Tom in trouble at the supper table?

options.A: He tells Aunt Polly that Tom skipped school.
options.B: He points out that Tom's shirt collar was sewn with black thread, not white.
options.C: He shows Aunt Polly the jam on Tom's hands.
options.D: He tells Aunt Polly that Tom was fighting the new boy.

1.shortAnswer: Aunt Polly says, "Every time I let him off, my conscience does hurt me so, and every time I hit him my old heart most breaks." What does this tell us about the conflict Aunt Polly feels about raising Tom? Use evidence from the chapter to support your answer.

multipleChoice.text: What is Tom's "magnificent inspiration" for getting out of whitewashing?

options.A: He pays the other boys to do the work for him.
options.B: He convinces Aunt Polly to let him off the hook.
options.C: He makes the work seem so fun that other boys beg to do it and pay him for the privilege.
options.D: He paints so fast that he finishes before anyone notices.

multipleChoice.text: By the end of the afternoon, Tom has collected twelve marbles, a kite, a dead rat, and many other treasures. What did the boys trade these items for?

options.A: A chance to play with Tom's toys
options.B: A turn whitewashing the fence
options.C: A peek at Tom's sore toe
options.D: A piece of Aunt Polly's jam

2.shortAnswer: The narrator says Tom discovered "a great law of human nature — that in order to make someone want something, all you have to do is make it hard to get." Give an example from your own life or from the world around you where this law is true. Explain how it works.

multipleChoice.text: Why does Tom deliberately tell the teacher he was talking to Huckleberry Finn?

options.A: He wants to impress the other students with his bravery.
options.B: He knows it will get him punished and sent to sit with the girls — next to Becky.
options.C: He is too honest to lie to the teacher.
options.D: He wants Huckleberry to get in trouble too.

multipleChoice.text: How does Becky first respond when Tom slides a peach toward her on the desk?

options.A: She eats it immediately.
options.B: She thanks him quietly.
options.C: She pushes it away.
options.D: She gives it to another student.

3.shortAnswer: Tom gets himself whipped and sent to the girls' side on purpose. What does this decision reveal about Tom's character? Is he foolish, brave, romantic, or something else? Explain your thinking using details from the chapter.

multipleChoice.text: What mistake does Tom make that ruins his engagement to Becky?

options.A: He forgets her name.
options.B: He accidentally mentions that he was engaged to Amy Lawrence before.
options.C: He refuses to kiss her.
options.D: He tells the other boys about their secret.

multipleChoice.text: After Becky pushes Tom away and cries, Tom offers her his most prized possession — a brass knob from an andiron. What does Becky do with it?

options.A: She accepts it gratefully.
options.B: She puts it in her pocket.
options.C: She throws it out the window.
options.D: She knocks it to the floor.

4.shortAnswer: Both Tom and Becky are stubborn in this chapter — neither one wants to be the first to apologize. Describe a moment when pride gets in the way of reconciliation between them. Why is it so hard for them to make up?

multipleChoice.text: Why do Tom and Huck go to the graveyard at midnight?

options.A: To dig for buried treasure
options.B: To test a dead cat cure for warts
options.C: To visit the grave of a friend
options.D: To play a prank on the town

multipleChoice.text: What actually happens at the graveyard?

options.A: The boys find buried treasure under a tombstone.
options.B: Dr. Robinson, Muff Potter, and Injun Joe dig up a body, and Injun Joe murders the doctor.
options.C: A ghost appears and frightens the boys away.
options.D: The boys accidentally knock over a gravestone and get caught.

5.shortAnswer: After Injun Joe kills Dr. Robinson, he places the knife in Muff Potter's hand and waits for Potter to wake up. Why does Joe do this? What does this tell us about Injun Joe as a character?

multipleChoice.text: Why do Tom and Huck decide to write their oath and sign it in blood instead of just shaking hands?

options.A: They think a written, blood-signed oath is more serious and binding.
options.B: Their teacher told them that all promises must be in writing.
options.C: They want to practice their handwriting.
options.D: They are copying something they saw in church.

multipleChoice.text: What happens to Tom after the night in the graveyard?

options.A: He feels proud of his adventure and brags to friends.
options.B: He is consumed by guilt, talks in his sleep, and can barely function.
options.C: He immediately tells Aunt Polly the truth.
options.D: He forgets about the murder and moves on with his life.

6.shortAnswer: Tom and Huck swear to "keep mum" and never tell what they saw. Do you think this is the right decision? Why or why not? Consider both the dangers of telling and the consequences of staying silent.

multipleChoice.text: Why doesn't Muff Potter defend himself when he is arrested?

options.A: He knows Injun Joe will kill him if he talks.
options.B: He was knocked unconscious during the murder and doesn't remember what really happened.
options.C: He is protecting Tom and Huck.
options.D: He confesses because he feels guilty about the grave robbing.

multipleChoice.text: What nearly gives away Tom's secret at the breakfast table?

options.A: Tom accidentally draws a picture of the graveyard.
options.B: Sid reports that Tom has been talking in his sleep about blood and telling secrets.
options.C: Huck comes to the house and asks to speak to Tom.
options.D: Aunt Polly finds the blood oath in Tom's pocket.

7.shortAnswer: Tom visits Muff Potter in jail and brings him small gifts. Why does Tom do this? What does it reveal about the "gnawing" effect the secret is having on his conscience?

multipleChoice.text: What is the main reason Tom decides to run away and become a pirate?

options.A: He wants to find buried treasure on the island.
options.B: He feels that nobody loves him and the world has treated him unfairly.
options.C: Aunt Polly told him to leave home.
options.D: He is running from Injun Joe.

multipleChoice.text: Why does Joe Harper want to run away?

options.A: He failed a test at school and is afraid of being punished.
options.B: His mother whipped him for something he didn't do.
options.C: He wants to become a famous explorer.
options.D: Tom dared him to do it.

8.shortAnswer: Tom, Joe, and Huck each have different reasons for running away. Compare their reasons. Whose reason seems the most serious, and whose seems the most dramatic? Explain.

multipleChoice.text: What do the boys hear booming across the river, and what does it mean?

options.A: Thunder from an approaching storm
options.B: Cannon fire — the town is searching for their drowned bodies
options.C: Fireworks celebrating the Fourth of July
options.D: A steamboat horn signaling danger

multipleChoice.text: How do the boys feel when they realize the town thinks they are dead?

options.A: Terrified and guilty
options.B: Proud and thrilled — they are the talk of the whole town
options.C: Angry that nobody came to find them
options.D: Bored and ready to go home

9.shortAnswer: At the end of the chapter, Tom sneaks away from camp and writes something on two pieces of sycamore bark. Why do you think the author doesn't tell us yet what Tom wrote? How does this create suspense?

multipleChoice.text: What does Tom learn while hiding under Aunt Polly's bed?

options.A: That Injun Joe has been captured
options.B: That the funeral will be held Sunday morning and the whole town believes the boys are dead
options.C: That Becky Thatcher has moved away
options.D: That Sid has told everyone about the blood oath

multipleChoice.text: What does Tom do just before leaving his aunt's house?

options.A: He leaves the sycamore bark message on the table.
options.B: He wakes Aunt Polly to tell her he's alive.
options.C: He almost leaves the bark message but changes his mind, puts it back in his pocket, and kisses Aunt Polly instead.
options.D: He takes food from the kitchen for the other boys.

10.shortAnswer: The boys walk into their own funeral and shock the entire congregation. Why is this moment the "proudest moment" of Tom's life? What does this tell us about what Tom values most?

multipleChoice.text: How does Tom explain knowing all the details of the conversation Aunt Polly had with Mrs. Harper?

options.A: He admits he was hiding under the bed.
options.B: He says Sid told him everything.
options.C: He claims he saw it all in a prophetic dream.
options.D: He says Huck Finn overheard it and told him.

multipleChoice.text: What does Aunt Polly find in the pocket of Tom's old jacket?

options.A: The blood oath from the graveyard
options.B: A piece of sycamore bark that says they are alive and only off being pirates
options.C: A letter from Becky Thatcher
options.D: A handful of stolen coins

11.shortAnswer: Aunt Polly calls the sycamore bark message proof that Tom loved her. Tom originally planned to leave it but changed his mind. Does Tom deserve the credit Aunt Polly gives him? Why or why not?

multipleChoice.text: Why does Mr. Dobbins become angry with the class?

options.A: Someone cheated on a test.
options.B: Someone tore a page in his private book.
options.C: Tom started a fight during class.
options.D: The students refused to do their homework.

multipleChoice.text: What does Tom do when Mr. Dobbins asks Becky if she tore the book?

options.A: He stays silent and lets her take the punishment.
options.B: He points at Alfred Temple.
options.C: He jumps to his feet and says he did it.
options.D: He tells the teacher it was an accident.

12.shortAnswer: Tom takes a whipping he doesn't deserve to protect Becky. How is this moment different from his earlier "showing off" to impress her? What has changed about Tom's feelings or motivations?

multipleChoice.text: What is the boys' plan for getting revenge on Mr. Dobbins?

options.A: They write rude things about him on the blackboard.
options.B: They arrange for a cat to be lowered from the attic to snatch his wig off his head.
options.C: They lock him out of the schoolhouse.
options.D: They put a snake in his desk drawer.

multipleChoice.text: Why does the audience laugh even harder when the cat removes Mr. Dobbins's wig?

options.A: The cat is wearing a tiny hat.
options.B: The sign-painter's boy had gilded (painted gold) the teacher's bald head while he napped.
options.C: Mr. Dobbins doesn't notice and keeps teaching.
options.D: The wig lands on a student's head.

13.shortAnswer: The chapter describes students performing recitations, essays, and demonstrations on Examination Day. How is Examination Day different from how we test students today? Which approach do you think is better, and why?

multipleChoice.text: Why does Tom join the Cadets of Temperance?

options.A: He believes strongly in their cause.
options.B: He is attracted by their fancy uniforms and red sashes.
options.C: Aunt Polly makes him join.
options.D: Becky Thatcher is a member.

multipleChoice.text: What happens to the town's religious revival by the time Tom recovers from his second bout of measles?

options.A: It has grown even stronger.
options.B: It has completely faded — the boys are back to their old ways.
options.C: It has spread to neighboring towns.
options.D: The minister has left town.

14.shortAnswer: Tom discovers that "to promise not to do a thing is the surest way in the world to make a body want to go and do that very thing." How does this connect to the "great law of human nature" Tom discovered in Chapter 2 (the whitewashing)? Are these the same idea or different?

multipleChoice.text: What is the most surprising moment at Muff Potter's trial?

options.A: Muff Potter confesses on the stand.
options.B: Injun Joe breaks down and admits what he did.
options.C: Potter's lawyer calls Tom Sawyer as a witness.
options.D: The jury finds Potter not guilty before hearing any testimony.

multipleChoice.text: What happens immediately after Tom describes seeing Injun Joe stab the doctor?

options.A: The jury convicts Potter.
options.B: Injun Joe crashes through a window and escapes.
options.C: The judge declares a mistrial.
options.D: Huck Finn runs out of the courtroom.

15.shortAnswer: Tom must have known that testifying would put him in danger from Injun Joe. Why does he do it anyway? What has finally become stronger than his fear?

multipleChoice.text: According to Tom, where is treasure usually buried?

options.A: In banks and safe-deposit boxes
options.B: Under haunted houses, dead trees, and on islands
options.C: At the bottom of rivers
options.D: In church basements

multipleChoice.text: Why do the boys decide they need to come back to the dead tree at midnight?

options.A: Ghosts only appear at midnight.
options.B: They need to see where the shadow of the dead branch falls at midnight to know where to dig.
options.C: The treasure is enchanted and can only be found at night.
options.D: They don't want anyone to see them digging.

16.shortAnswer: Tom and Huck are full of superstitions about treasure hunting — incantations, witches, midnight shadows. How do these superstitions affect the way they approach the treasure hunt? Do the superstitions help them or hold them back?

multipleChoice.text: What terrifying discovery do Tom and Huck make while hiding upstairs in the haunted house?

options.A: The house is actually occupied by a family.
options.B: The "deaf and dumb Spaniard" is actually Injun Joe in disguise.
options.C: There is a trap door leading to an underground tunnel.
options.D: A pack of wild dogs has made the house their den.

multipleChoice.text: Why does Injun Joe become suspicious that someone else has been in the haunted house?

options.A: He finds footprints in the dust.
options.B: He sees the boys' pick and shovel with fresh dirt on them.
options.C: He hears the boys whispering upstairs.
options.D: He notices the front door has been left open.

17.shortAnswer: Injun Joe says the treasure needs to go to "Number Two — under the cross" and mentions that his planned "job" is about revenge, not money. What do these details tell us about what might happen next in the story? Make a prediction and explain your reasoning.

multipleChoice.text: How does Tom figure out that "Number Two" is a room in a tavern?

options.A: He finds a map in Injun Joe's hideout.
options.B: He asks around town and discovers that the cheaper tavern has a mysterious, always-locked Room Number Two.
options.C: Huck overhears Injun Joe talking about it.
options.D: The sheriff tells him.

multipleChoice.text: What does Tom find when he sneaks into Room Number Two?

options.A: The treasure box sitting on a table
options.B: An empty room with a cross scratched on the wall
options.C: Injun Joe, passed out drunk on the floor
options.D: A note from Injun Joe warning him to stay away

18.shortAnswer: Tom and Huck take enormous risks to track down the treasure — sneaking into a room where a murderer is sleeping. Is the treasure worth the danger? What would you do in their situation?

multipleChoice.text: What causes Tom and Becky to become lost in the cave?

options.A: A sudden flood blocks their path.
options.B: They wander away from the group while exploring and can't find their way back.
options.C: Someone locks the cave entrance behind them.
options.D: Their map of the cave is wrong.

multipleChoice.text: What is the most frightening moment for the children in this chapter?

options.A: They see a bear inside the cave.
options.B: They realize Tom didn't leave any marks to find the way back.
options.C: A rockslide blocks their exit.
options.D: They run out of food.

19.shortAnswer: When Becky realizes they are lost, she says, "We're lost! We never can get out of this awful place! Oh, why did we ever leave the others!" How does Becky's reaction compare to Tom's? What does this scene reveal about how each character handles fear?

multipleChoice.text: Why does Huck follow the two men instead of going to get Tom?

options.A: He is too scared to move.
options.B: He is afraid that if he leaves to get Tom, the men will disappear with the treasure forever.
options.C: Tom told him to follow the men no matter what.
options.D: He wants to confront Injun Joe himself.

multipleChoice.text: What does Huck do when he hears Injun Joe's plan to hurt the Widow Douglas?

options.A: He confronts Injun Joe directly.
options.B: He runs to the Welshman's house and warns him.
options.C: He hides and waits for the men to leave.
options.D: He goes back to Tom's house for help.

20.shortAnswer: Huck is terrified of Injun Joe, yet he risks his life to save the Widow Douglas. What motivates Huck to act despite his fear? What does this moment reveal about who Huck really is underneath his rough exterior?

multipleChoice.text: What happens when Tom and Becky's last candle burns out?

options.A: Tom lights a match and they keep going.
options.B: They are plunged into total darkness with no way to see.
options.C: They find another candle left behind by tourists.
options.D: Moonlight comes through a crack in the ceiling.

multipleChoice.text: Who does Tom see when he explores a side passage with his kite string?

options.A: One of the search party members
options.B: Muff Potter
options.C: Injun Joe, holding a candle
options.D: Huckleberry Finn

21.shortAnswer: Becky tells Tom to go on without her — she is ready to give up and die. Tom refuses and keeps searching. What does Tom's refusal to give up tell us about how he has changed since the beginning of the book? Compare this Tom to the Tom of Chapter 1.

multipleChoice.text: How does Tom finally find a way out of the cave?

options.A: He follows the sound of running water to a river exit.
options.B: He sees a tiny speck of daylight at the end of a passage and crawls toward it.
options.C: He finds a map scratched into the cave wall.
options.D: Huck Finn finds the cave entrance and calls to him.

multipleChoice.text: What has happened in the town while Tom and Becky were lost?

options.A: The town has given up searching and is in mourning.
options.B: Injun Joe has been captured.
options.C: The school has been closed permanently.
options.D: Aunt Polly has moved away.

22.shortAnswer: When Tom escapes and looks out at the Mississippi River, he has been in total darkness for three days. Describe what you think that moment felt like for Tom. Use sensory details (what he sees, feels, hears) in your answer.

multipleChoice.text: What has happened to Injun Joe when the townspeople unlock the sealed cave?

options.A: He has escaped through another exit.
options.B: He is found alive but weak.
options.C: He is found dead near the door, having starved to death.
options.D: He has built a shelter and is living inside the cave.

multipleChoice.text: Where do Tom and Huck find the treasure?

options.A: In Room Number Two at the tavern
options.B: Buried under the cross mark inside the cave, exactly where Injun Joe had hidden it
options.C: In the Widow Douglas's woodshed
options.D: Under the haunted house hearthstone

23.shortAnswer: Tom says he feels "an overwhelming, bone-deep sense of relief" when he learns Injun Joe is dead. He also feels pity because he knows what it's like to be trapped in the cave. Can a person feel relief and pity at the same time? Explain, using details from the chapter.

multipleChoice.text: Why does Huck run away from the Widow Douglas's house?

options.A: He is afraid of Injun Joe's friends.
options.B: He cannot stand the rules of civilized life — eating with forks, wearing shoes, going to church.
options.C: The other children at school bully him.
options.D: He wants to find more treasure in the cave.

multipleChoice.text: How does Tom convince Huck to go back to the Widow Douglas?

options.A: He promises Huck more treasure.
options.B: He tells Huck that he can't join Tom Sawyer's Gang unless he is respectable.
options.C: He threatens to tell the Widow where Huck is hiding.
options.D: He offers Huck his share of the treasure.

24.shortAnswer: The book ends with Huck agreeing to "try" civilization so he can join Tom's robber gang. Do you think Huck will last? Why or why not? Use what you know about Huck's character from the whole book to support your prediction.
